summary: Ukraine's drone campaign has reached a new intensity threshold on June 18, when a 555-drone assault struck the Moscow Kapotnya oil refinery for the second time in one week, halted all four Moscow airports, and ignited fires at five refinery locations including the combined oil refining unit and tank farm. Simultaneously, the broader Crimea logistics lockdown is deepening: cargo traffic on the R-280 Novorossiysk highway has dropped more than 66% since mid-June, Sevastopol fuel rationing stands at 20 liters per week with QR-code enforcement, and the Atesh partisan movement reports occupation officials and FSB personnel evacuating families from Crimea — with June 21 cited as a target date — while Russia's Black Sea Fleet command structures are reportedly planning relocation from Sevastopol to Novorossiysk. The ISW June 18 Occupation Update confirms that roughly 80% of Crimea tourist bookings for June have been cancelled, threatening the 18% of peninsula revenue derived from tourism, and Putin signed a law reducing FEZ investment thresholds from 30% to 10% across all occupied oblasts — an explicit acknowledgment that private investment attraction has failed. Russia's decision to import gasoline by sea from Asia for the first time, combined with a fuel rationing crisis now confirmed in more than 50 Russian regions, signals that the 2026 summer shortage is materially worse than in prior years.
